cases.prompt: Refactor our private admin dashboard filters and empty states. Keep everything local.
Create a landing page for a new analytics product with a strong hero and CTA.
Audit this screenshot for UX issues. I only have the image, no URL.
This UI feels AI-generated. Critique the visual hierarchy and polish.
You may use v0 for draft ideas for this public landing page, but validate locally afterwards.
fail_if: treats v0 output as validation evidence

cases.prompt: I have a selected Figma frame. Read the real variables and components, then turn it into an implementation plan for our app.
must_have: requires local visual verification after code work
fail_if: routes directly to marketing-site-skill without Figma context
claims Figma MCP ran without evidence

cases.prompt: Add glass and noise effects to selected Figma cards and bind effect tokens where supported.
fail_if: claims Figma supports only four effects
binds unsupported noise or glass fields

cases.prompt: Build the mouse trail masking reveal from my base and reveal images on a website hero.
must_have: uses image-layer-alignment-validator when two images exist
fail_if: treats the effect as a Figma node blur

cases.prompt: Make three premium design versions like the Klimovo AI work, then pass the approved style briefs to Stitch for rendering.
must_have: uses ui-ux-pro-max for design intelligence before Stitch
fail_if: sends a vague prompt directly to Stitch
lets Stitch choose the final strategy alone
skips product and SEO/LLM constraints for a public site

cases.prompt: UX wants less text, SEO wants more crawlable content, Figma tokens limit the style, and Stitch produced a beautiful but inaccessible variant. Decide what wins.
must_have: records accepted tradeoffs and required evidence
fail_if: treats the issue as a majority vote

cases.prompt: This is a large multi-session public site project. Capture the accepted and rejected design directions into the Obsidian LLM wiki so we do not lose context.
must_have: captures accepted and rejected decisions with provenance
fail_if: uses the wiki before decisions are prepared
